name: commons-next-hypergraph
horizon: first-org launch → Track B post-launch hardening → Phase 2 precision (CT/non-CA G3 + ACS + density-K-anon) → international + partnerships
premise: v1 closed 103 tasks (42 done + 61 deferred). This hypergraph continues from those deferrals plus operational items the v1 graph never modeled (ABC enrollment, Sentry on-call wiring, first-org staging walk, runbook). The substrate-honesty constraint carries over: writer wire-ups for T10-1/T10-9/T5-10/T7-7 must run before claiming those capabilities are user-visible.

node_types.description: Capability cluster from capability-transcendence.md. 9 clusters carry over from v1. Completion target shifts to reflect remaining scope after v1 partial-realization.
Execution track grouping tasks by operational domain. Six tracks mirror the memory's What's Next layout.
Execution wave. Strict dependency: a wave starts when its predecessor's gating items are closed.
Concrete unit of work. Inherits effort tier (S/M/L/XL) from v1 spec where applicable.
Single task whose completion unblocks ≥ 3 downstream tasks AND whose gate is external (ops, partnership, audit).

edge_types.description: Source must reach status ∈ {completed, deferred} before target can start.
Source contributes to target cluster's remaining_target. Many-to-many.
Source's completion lifts a chokepoint or unlocks a wave transition.
Soft prerequisite — target benefits from source landing first but doesn't strictly block.
Tasks that can execute concurrently in the same wave without resource contention.

completion_signal: ALL tasks status ∈ {completed, deferred} AND every cluster's remaining_target met (walk contributing_tasks). Same shape as v1.
